overview: SFURTI (Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries) is a programme of the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, Government of India, aimed at organizing traditional industries and artisans into collectives to enhance their competitiveness, ensure long-term sustainability, and improve artisanal income through infrastructure development, technology upgradation, training, product innovation, and market linkage support. The scheme focuses on Khadi, Village Industries, and Coir sectors, promoting collective business enterprises and convergence with public-private partnerships and e-commerce initiatives.
objectives: To organize traditional industries and artisans into collectives for competitiveness and long-term sustainability
To provide sustained employment for traditional industry artisans and producers
To enhance marketability of products through new products, design intervention, improved packaging, and marketing infrastructure
To equip artisans with improved skills and capabilities through training and exposure visits
To promote collective business enterprises of traditional artisans and producers
To make provision for common facilities and improved tools and equipment for optimum utilization of infrastructure
To set up multi-product clusters with integrated value chains and market-driven approaches
To strengthen cluster governance systems for equitable distribution of surplus
To promote traditional skills, advanced processes, and new models of public-private partnerships
To focus on uncovered and aspirational districts for setting up collectives with emphasis on endangered arts and crafts
To support traditional artisans from disadvantaged communities like SCs, STs, women, etc.
To ensure convergence from design stage with cluster formation and operations
To identify target customers and develop product lines to meet their needs, with focus on natural, eco-friendly, ethically sourced products
To develop specific product lines and undertake brand unification exercises
To shift from supply-driven to market-driven models with right branding, product mix, positioning, and pricing
To tap e-commerce as a major marketing channel for E-Retail presence
To invest in product design, quality improvement, R&D, and emerging technologies to standardize quality benchmarks
To promote latest technological advancements for local-to-global reach in line with 'Atma Nirbhar Bharat'
To establish collectives with focus on Industry 4.0, Blockchain for traceability, digitization, effluent treatment, fire safety, green energy, and knowledge repositories
To encourage and promote green and sustainable products and processes
eligibility: Any Implementing Agency (IA) interested in submitting a proposal under SFURTI Scheme shall first submit a Concept note along with necessary documents on the SFURTI portal. The IA must be registered as an Implementing Agency on the portal. Proposals are submitted online to Nodal Agencies (NAs), which include KVIC for Khadi and Village Industry clusters, Coir Board for Coir-based clusters, and other national-level institutions with sectoral expertise in Traditional Industries, competence in cluster/collective development, project management, and facilitation of market access, operating in the field for at least five years. The IA must authenticate the list of artisans, form a project-specific SPV as a 'for profit' entity, ensure land availability for CFC construction (owned or leased for 15+ years), and confirm IA/SPV's share of 10% (5% for NER and Hilly areas).
benefits: Support includes creation of Common Facility Centres (CFCs) and worksheds with IP-enabled CCTV cameras, procurement of machineries, working capital and raw material banks (RMBs), tools and technological upgradation (e.g., charkha upgradation, tool-kit distribution), warehousing facilities, training centers, skill development and capacity building, machine handling and maintenance training, institution development, exposure visits, market promotion initiatives, design and product development, participation in seminars/workshops on technology upgradation, brand building and promotion campaigns, new media marketing, e-Commerce initiatives, innovation, R&D initiatives, technological upgradation, and developing institutional linkages. Financial support includes working capital up to 20% of project cost for raw material procurement, direct wages, electricity and water costs, provided as a revolving fund for initial production cycle, recoupable from product sales.
financial_support: The scheme provides financial support for hard interventions including creation of Common Facility Centres (CFCs), worksheds, procurement of machineries, working capital and raw material banks (RMBs), tools and technological upgradation, warehousing, and training centers. Working Capital of up to 20% of project cost may be budgeted for raw material procurement, direct wages, electricity and water costs, provided as a revolving fund for the initial production cycle, to be recouped from product sales. Financial assistance is disbursed to Implementing Agencies (IAs) through Nodal Agencies (NAs) on recommendation of Technical Agencies (TAs) to ensure time-bound project completion.
application_process: 1. Register as an Implementing Agency on the SFURTI portal to create user ID and password. 2. Log in to the portal and download the Instruction/Guidelines for filling Concept Application and Scorecard. 3. Select 'Scorecard' from the dashboard. 4. Fill the basic details form and save. 5. Select the 'Edit' option to proceed after saving Cluster Details. 6. Upload the duly filled Concept Application Form (download format from dashboard if new applicant). 7. Fill details against each indicator, selecting options and uploading supporting evidence simultaneously before moving to the next indicator. 8. Upload supporting evidence for each indicator by choosing and uploading files (PDF, max 5 MB per indicator). 9. For indicators requiring multiple options, select multiple checkboxes as per instructions. 10. View the scorecard from the 'View Scorecard' tab. 11. Select the preferred Nodal Agency from the drop-down list and click the forward button to submit the scorecard with application and uploaded documents to the concerned Nodal Agency. 12. Upon forwarding, a submission intimation message is displayed; the scorecard can be printed if required.
required_documents: Project Initiation Reports
Project Completion Reports
Project Evaluation/Monitoring reports
Certificate of Incorporation (with renewal if applicable)
Self-attested year-wise project summary report
Self-attested village-wise number of beneficiary summary report
Self-attested summary report indicating types of interventions undertaken
Audited Statement of last three years (Income & Expenditure Account)
Audited Statement of last three years (Income & Expenditure Account and Balance Sheet)
Utilisation Certificate (for Completed Projects)
Grant Letter (for Ongoing Projects)
Self-attested summary report mentioning interventions carried out in the area/for artisans
Self-attested comparative table indicating present income of artisans and break-up/justification of income increase
Registered lease deed (if land is leased by SPV)
Registered sale or lease deed (long term of 15 years or more) in IA/SPV's name for land availability
MOU/MoA and other related agreements between IA and SPV, and IA and NA
Concept note along with necessary documents
Detailed Project Report (DPR) as per standard template
Proposal Application Form format
Instructions For Scorecard
User Manual For Filling Online Scorecard
implementing_agency: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
caveats: Working Capital support as a revolving fund must be recouped from the sale of products
Need for Working Capital expenses must be clearly spelt out in business plans
Assistance for raw material procurement may be leveraged with financial institutions for enhanced credit
Total number of clusters assigned to a Technical Agency will not exceed 50 at a time
In case of significant shortfall in progress or furnishing of incorrect information, support/empanelment of Nodal Agencies may be terminated mid-term with approval of Scheme Steering Committee
Existing proposals of a terminated Nodal Agency will be transferred to another Nodal Agency with approval of Project Management Committee
Grants-in-Aid to new Nodal Agencies are subject to Terms and Conditions in Annexure-4
Any representative of TA or IA who has submitted a proposal for SFURTI to the Nodal Agency should not be part of the Project Screening Committee to avoid conflict of interest
Minutes of the Project Screening Committee must be enclosed along with the DPR
The incorporated SPV must be a 'for profit' entity
IA/SPV must confirm availability of 10% share (5% for NER and Hilly areas)
Program fund management: Nodal Agency must ensure timely disbursement of funds to IAs on recommendation of TA
Business Plan and Projected income of artisans in DPR must be validated by Chartered Financial Analysts (CFA)/Chartered Accountant (CA)
Representative of MSME (DI) will be a member of the Tender Committee
